name: AppCert DLLs Registry Modification Detected
description: Detects modifications (creation, setting, or deletion) to registry keys under `\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\Session Manager\AppCertDlls\`. This is a highly impactful persistence and privilege escalation technique where adversaries can inject malicious DLLs that are automatically loaded into every process that utilizes `CreateProcess` APIs, providing broad access to system processes.

prompts.prompt: A critical security event has been detected on the host **{computer_name}**: the registry key **{target_object}**, which is a critical **AppCert DLLs** key, was modified by the process **{image}** under the user **{user}**. This activity is a strong indicator of an **AppCert DLLs** persistence attack (**MITRE ATT&CK T1546.009**), a technique used to inject a malicious DLL into every new process created on the system. The event details show the new value **{details}**. This is a high-confidence alert for a security incident and requires immediate, high-priority investigation. Treat this as a confirmed compromise until proven otherwise.

parameters.name: target_object
parameters.description: The full path of the registry key or value that was modified.

parameters.name: image
parameters.description: The process that performed the registry modification.

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: The user account that performed the modification.

parameters.name: details
parameters.description: The new value written to the registry key, containing the DLL path.

parameters.name: computer_name
parameters.description: The name of the computer where the event occurred.
